assumptions: couplings are finite symmetric non-negative graph weights
cascade severity is measured by largest component loss
passing the fixture is not operational security evidence
specs.canonical_statement: Cascading-failure wording is bounded to weighted-graph percolation tests over finite coupling networks.
specs.claim_boundary: simulator-only robustness boundary; not operational security evidence
executable_validation_targets: verify thresholded graph largest component fraction is bounded
verify targeted removal reduces largest component fraction
verify invalid coupling matrices are rejected
specs.section_path: Structural Overview (by Domain) > Computational Unifier. > IV. System Robustness and Failure Modes Analysis
validation_targets: verify thresholded graph largest component fraction is bounded
verify targeted removal reduces largest component fraction
verify invalid coupling matrices are rejected

assumptions: sigma is finite positive and not exactly at the critical point
recovery time is a simulator observable, not a clinical prognosis
passing the fixture is not operational security evidence
specs.canonical_statement: Critical-slowing wording is bounded to finite recovery-time scaling as sigma approaches the phase-transition point.
specs.claim_boundary: simulator-only robustness boundary; not operational security evidence
executable_validation_targets: verify recovery time increases near sigma criticality
verify far-from-critical control has lower recovery time
null_controls: far-from-transition control must reduce recovery time
specs.section_path: Structural Overview (by Domain) > Computational Unifier. > IV. System Robustness and Failure Modes Analysis
validation_targets: verify recovery time increases near sigma criticality
verify far-from-critical control has lower recovery time

assumptions: attack is a simulator stressor parameter, not an operational exploit
MS-QEC redundancy monotonically improves success under finite exposure
passing the fixture is not operational security evidence
specs.canonical_statement: Decoherence-attack wording is bounded to an MS-QEC success-probability fixture with explicit redundancy and correction-strength controls.
specs.claim_boundary: simulator-only robustness boundary; not operational security evidence
executable_validation_targets: verify MS-QEC success probability is bounded in the unit interval
verify redundancy reduces failure probability relative to unprotected control
verify invalid redundancy or correction strength is rejected
null_controls: unprotected-control failure must exceed protected failure
specs.section_path: Structural Overview (by Domain) > Computational Unifier. > IV. System Robustness and Failure Modes Analysis
validation_targets: verify MS-QEC success probability is bounded in the unit interval
verify redundancy reduces failure probability relative to unprotected control
verify invalid redundancy or correction strength is rejected

summary.policy: P0R06215-P0R06217 are promoted as source-covered simulator robustness specifications only. Passing fixtures is not operational security evidence.
